ingredients.entityContext: Tully's Coffee at Akihabara UDX is located inside the UDX complex and is depicted in Steins;Gate 0 as a meeting spot. Characters including Okabe and Maho use cafe settings near the UDX for tense conversations about the Amadeus AI project.

ingredients.name: Wakoshi Sta.
ingredients.address: 1-chōme-1 Maruyamadai, Wako, Saitama 351-0112, Japan

ingredients.name: Wakoshi Station
ingredients.address: 4 Honchō, Wako, Saitama 351-0114, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Wakoshi Station appears in Steins;Gate 0 as a location tied to disturbing events in alternate timelines. The station area around Wako, Saitama is depicted in sequences related to the darker alternate-timeline storylines explored in the series.

ingredients.name: Yanagimori Shrine
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-25-1 Kanda Sudacho, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0041, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Yanagimori Shrine is the real-world basis for Yanabayashi Shrine, a recurring location in Steins;Gate. In Steins;Gate 0, Sion and Kagari shelter at the shrine following Kagari's memory loss, making it a key setting in the Kagari arc.

ingredients.name: ABC-MART Ikebukuro ANNEX Shop
ingredients.address: 1F, 1 Chome-15-1 Higashiikebukuro, Toshima City, Tokyo 170-0013, Japan

ingredients.name: AKIHABARA KOTOBUKIYA
ingredients.address: Okajima, 1 Chome-8-8 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021
ingredients.entityContext: Kotobukiya Akihabara is a major hobby and figure shop on Chuo-Dori that forms part of the authentic Akihabara backdrop in Steins;Gate 0, appearing in street scenes that establish the otaku district environment Okabe inhabits.

ingredients.name: Akibaoo 2nd Shop
ingredients.address: バウハウス 1F, 1 Chome-8-10 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan

ingredients.name: Akihabara Gamers
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-14-7 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Akihabara Gamers is one of the recognizable storefronts along Chuo-Dori that appears in establishing shots of Akihabara throughout both Steins;Gate and Steins;Gate 0, grounding the anime's setting in the real otaku shopping street.

ingredients.name: Akihabara Radio Kaikan
ingredients.type: ショッピング モール
categories: ショッピング モール
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-15-16 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Akihabara Radio Kaikan is the building depicted as the IBN 5100 research conference site in Steins;Gate, and the crash point of the time machine in the original series. In Steins;Gate 0, it remains a key landmark framing Okabe's walks through the Electric Town as he grapples with Kurisu's death.

ingredients.name: Akihabara Station
ingredients.address: 1 Chome Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Akihabara Station is one of the most frequently depicted locations in both Steins;Gate and Steins;Gate 0. The Electric Town exit stairs and plaza appear in pivotal scenes, including Okabe's emotionally charged walks through the area and crowd sequences tied to his PTSD-like episodes in S;G 0.

ingredients.name: Akihabara Station Electric Town Exit Midori Window
ingredients.localName: 秋葉原駅 電気街口みどりの窓口
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-17 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Electric Town Exit ticket window area of Akihabara Station is one of the recognizable entry points to the shopping district depicted in Steins;Gate 0, appearing in scenes where Okabe arrives or departs from Akihabara.

ingredients.name: Akihabara Station Electric Town Exit [West Plaza]
ingredients.localName: 秋葉原駅電気街口【西側広場】
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-17-18 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The west plaza of Akihabara Station's Electric Town exit is shown repeatedly in Steins;Gate 0 as the gateway to Akihabara's otaku district. Okabe passes through this area in multiple episodes while tracking leads related to Amadeus and the mysterious organization pursuing Kagari.

ingredients.name: Akihabara UDX
ingredients.type: ビジネス センター
categories: ビジネス センター
ingredients.address: 4 Chome-14-1 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Akihabara UDX appears in Steins;Gate 0 as part of the modern commercial complex adjacent to the station. The elevated walkways and building exterior are visible in background shots during several street-level sequences near the Electric Town exit.

ingredients.name: Akihabara UDX 2F JR side deck
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒101-0021 Tokyo, Chiyoda City, Sotokanda, 4 Chome−14−14 2F
ingredients.entityContext: The 2F JR-side deck of Akihabara UDX is depicted in Steins;Gate 0 as an elevated vantage point over the station area. This open-air walkway appears in scenes where characters survey the street below, matching the anime's distinctive top-down city views.

ingredients.name: Animate Akihabara
ingredients.address: 4 Chome-3-1 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Animate Akihabara is one of the flagship anime merchandise stores on Chuo-Dori that appears in background street shots in Steins;Gate 0, part of the recognizable Electric Town shopping strip where Okabe and the lab members spend their daily lives.

ingredients.name: BicCamera AKIBA Store
ingredients.address: 4 Chome-1-１ Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: BicCamera AKIBA sits on Chuo-Dori at the southern end of Electric Town and appears in street-level background shots throughout Steins;Gate 0, contributing to the authentic Akihabara environment the anime depicts.

ingredients.name: Cafe Mai:lish
ingredients.localName: カフェ メイリッシュ
ingredients.address: Fh協和スクエア, 3 Chome-6-2 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Cafe Mai:lish is the real-world model for MayQueen+Nyan2, the maid cafe where Faris Nyannyan works in Steins;Gate and Steins;Gate 0. In S;G 0, Okabe and the lab members still visit the cafe as a rare moment of normalcy amid the darker storyline.

ingredients.name: Hijiri Bridge
ingredients.address: 4 Chome Kanda Surugadai, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0062, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Hijiri Bridge spans the Kanda River near Ochanomizu and appears in Steins;Gate and Steins;Gate 0 as a scenic Tokyo landmark. The bridge's arching form is visible in background shots of the broader area around the lab's Akihabara neighborhood.

ingredients.entityContext: Juntendo Hospital appears in Steins;Gate 0 in connection with medical and research scenes. The prominent university hospital building near Hongo is depicted in episodes dealing with Okabe's psychological state and the institutional backdrop of scientific research in the series.

ingredients.name: Kanda Fureai Bridge
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-8-7 Kanda Sakumacho, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0025, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kanda Fureai Bridge is a confirmed pilgrimage spot for Steins;Gate fans and appears in Steins;Gate 0's future war timeline sequences, where it is shown destroyed and collapsed onto the street below. The bridge also features in quieter scenes where Okabe and Ruka meet.
